experiences.intentions: Current active piece is T near the top in rotation 0. The stack is relatively low with no immediate danger, and there is open space beneath. A good sub-goal is to improve placement flexibility before committing, and rotating to the vertical-right T orientation can help thread into future notches while still high enough to reposition afterward. Candidate moves: left/right only shift horizontally without improving fit yet; soft/hard drop commits too early from the spawn area; no_op wastes a turn. rotate_right changes to a more useful orientation while preserving mobility.
